 {"id":"ft-arhlm","title":"[MEDIUM] MCP missing-golden panic still points operators at the wrong regeneration command","description":"Commit f6e7cc15 corrected the drift-path regeneration command, but the missing-golden panic still tells operators to run `UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 cargo test --test mcp_manifest_golden` (`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_manifest_golden.rs:200-205`). In this workspace, default features enable a much wider surface than the minimal review lane, so regenerating via that command can produce a different manifest than the intended `--no-default-features --features mcp,asupersync-runtime` path. This leaves the test with two conflicting operator instructions and can regenerate the wrong golden when the fixture is absent. Expected fix: make the missing-file panic use the same explicit feature-set command as the drift path and top-of-file documentation.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:58:05.098815Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:01:26.813721Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-b5rbd","title":"[HIGH] GUI resize increment saturation can overflow backend min-size arithmetic","description":"Commit 193a9ebb changes `ResizeIncrementCalculator` to saturate `base_width`/`base_height` at the u16 ceiling (`crates/frankenterm-gui/src/resize_increment_calculator.rs:15-35`). Multiple window backends then immediately compute `base + increment` in u16 without guarding overflow (`frankenterm/window/src/os/macos/window.rs:1410-1412`, `frankenterm/window/src/os/x11/window.rs:1892-1893`, `frankenterm/window/src/os/wayland/window.rs:915-916`). With large padding/border/tab-bar geometry, the new saturation can produce `base_width == u16::MAX`, so `base_width + x` overflows before min-size clamping. This turns an intended overflow hardening change into a runtime panic/wraparound path on oversized layouts. Expected fix: clamp stored base dimensions to `u16::MAX - increment` (or widen backend arithmetic) and add a regression test that asserts `base + increment` stays representable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:58:04.554054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:42:08.945504Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:42:08.945232Z","close_reason":"dup of ft-htf0i","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-b677n","title":"[MEDIUM] wgpu readback poll loop has no test coverage for timeout, disconnect, or error-propagation paths","description":"Commit 6f41130c (br-43bbl fix wgpu readback polling) rewrites the
readback wait in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:168-193
from:

\`\`\`rust
self.device.poll(PollType::Wait { submission_index: None, timeout: Some(WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT) })?;
receiver.recv_timeout(WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT)?...
\`\`\`

to a bounded poll loop:

\`\`\`rust
let deadline = Instant::now() + WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT;
loop {
    self.device.poll(wgpu::PollType::Poll)?;
    match receiver.recv_timeout(Duration::from_millis(10)) {
        Ok(result) => { result?; break; }
        Err(RecvTimeoutError::Timeout) if Instant::now() < deadline => continue,
        Err(RecvTimeoutError::Timeout) => return Err(...),
        Err(RecvTimeoutError::Disconnected) => return Err(...),
    }
}
\`\`\`

Concerns with the new implementation:

1. \`PollType::Poll\` is non-blocking by contract in wgpu 25. The loop
   relies on \`Poll\` actually driving the \`map_async\` callback forward.
   If a future wgpu point release changes \`Poll\` to be strictly
   \"dispatch-only\" (no callback invocation), the loop would always
   hit the 5s timeout. There is no test pinning this behavior.

2. The \`Disconnected\` branch is reachable only if the \`map_async\`
   closure drops without sending — that happens e.g., if wgpu drops
   the submission without invoking the callback (device lost, internal
   error). No test exercises this path.

3. \`Instant::now() + WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT\` panics on overflow
   (impossible in practice for 5s, but worth noting as a latent
   concern). Prefer \`checked_add\`.

4. Busy-wait cost: on the timeout path, the loop spins ~500 iterations
   (5000ms / 10ms) calling \`device.poll\` each time. The prior
   \`PollType::Wait\` blocked in the driver. This is a CPU-efficiency
   regression on cancellation/device-loss paths, not a correctness
   regression.

Impact: readback behavior on wgpu failure paths (device lost, long
delay, map_async disconnect) is silently unverified. A correctness
regression in \`PollType::Poll\` semantics between wgpu versions would
silently break screenshots and readback-based features.

Fix direction:
- Add a unit-level test with a mock/stub Device that controls
  \`poll()\` return timing and can force a Disconnected on the mpsc
  channel. Assert each of the four loop outcomes.
- Consider moving the readback loop into a small helper function
  that takes a \`Fn()\` poll closure + channel receiver, to make it
  unit-testable without a real GPU.
- If unit isolation is impractical, at minimum add an integration
  test that triggers a readback and asserts completion within a
  tight CPU budget (regression test for busy-wait blow-up).

Severity: medium — latent risk in a GPU-facing path that typically
works and has no automated coverage for failure modes.

Evidence: commit 6f41130c diff;
crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:168-193 at HEAD.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:25:33.419495Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:25:33.419495Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gpu","review","test-gap","testing","webgpu"]}
-{"id":"ft-cbeyl","title":"[MEDIUM] mcp_manifest_golden db-gated tool whitelist silently drifts when tool renamed or removed","description":"In commit 1bde42b3 (ft-nnfwn: freeze no-db mcp manifest surface),
\`mcp_manifest_matches_golden_without_db\` at
crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_manifest_golden.rs:254-316 hard-codes
three whitelists of tools/resources/templates expected to be db-gated:

\`\`\`rust
for expected_absent_tool in [
    \"wa.accounts\",
    \"wa.events\",
    \"wa.reserve\",
    \"wa.release\",
    \"wa.search\",
    \"wa.send\",
    \"wa.workflow_run\",
] {
    assert!(
        !no_db_tools.contains(expected_absent_tool),
        \"db-gated tool unexpectedly advertised without db_path: {expected_absent_tool}\"
    );
}
\`\`\`

The assertion only checks absence from \`no_db_tools\`. If a tool is
renamed (e.g., \`wa.accounts\` → \`wa.list_accounts\`) or removed entirely,
the whitelist entry silently stays true (\`!no_db_tools.contains(\"wa.accounts\")\`
is trivially true when \`wa.accounts\` no longer exists in either manifest).

The same drift pattern applies to the resource whitelist
(lines ~285, \`wa://accounts\`, \`wa://events\`, \`wa://reservations\`) and
the template whitelist (lines ~295, \`wa://accounts/{service}\` etc.).

Impact: the no-db subset guard gives false confidence. A tool could
accidentally become always-on (or be removed from db-gated registration)
without the test catching it.

Fix: for each whitelist entry, assert \`full_tools.contains(entry)\`
BEFORE checking \`!no_db_tools.contains(entry)\`. That way, if the
whitelisted tool disappears from the db-backed manifest, the test
fails and forces whoever made the change to update the whitelist
deliberately.

\`\`\`rust
for expected_absent_tool in [...] {
    assert!(
        full_tools.contains(expected_absent_tool),
        \"whitelist drift: {expected_absent_tool} no longer exists in db-backed manifest — update the test\"
    );
    assert!(
        !no_db_tools.contains(expected_absent_tool),
        \"db-gated tool unexpectedly advertised without db_path: {expected_absent_tool}\"
    );
}
\`\`\`

Severity rationale: medium — test-quality issue that masks regressions
in MCP tool surface categorization. Not a correctness bug in production
code.

Evidence: commit 1bde42b3 diff lines 262-316 of the new
\`mcp_manifest_matches_golden_without_db\` test body.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:24:49.960054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:24:49.960054Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","review","test-gap","testing"]}

is trivially true when \`wa.accounts\` no longer exists in either manifest).

The same drift pattern applies to the resource whitelist
(lines ~285, \`wa://accounts\`, \`wa://events\`, \`wa://reservations\`) and

Impact: the no-db subset guard gives false confidence. A tool could

whitelisted tool disappears from the db-backed manifest, the test

deliberately.

\`\`\`rust
for expected_absent_tool in [...] {

        full_tools.contains(expected_absent_tool),

code.

 {"id":"ft-d0ez0","title":"[EPIC] End-to-end swarm integration proof: demonstrate full observe-detect-react loop with live agents","description":"# Goal
Prove that FrankenTerm actually delivers on its core value proposition end-to-end: observing live AI agent panes, detecting state transitions, triggering automated workflows, and exposing actionable data through Robot Mode. Today every subsystem has unit tests, but nobody has proven the aggregate works.

# Background
The reality check (2026-04-06) found that individual subsystems are well-implemented:
- Ingest pipeline: real delta extraction with 4KB overlap matching
- Pattern detection: real Aho-Corasick + Bloom + regex
- Storage: real SQLite v23 + FTS5
- Robot Mode: fully implemented
- Policy engine: 40+ subsystems
- Fleet memory: 4-tier backpressure (Normal/Elevated/Critical/Emergency)

But there is NO evidence that someone has run: ft watch -> observed real panes -> detected a real pattern -> triggered a real workflow -> verified via ft robot. The individual subsystems are proven; the integration is not.

# Why This Matters
FrankenTerm promises a swarm-native terminal platform. Without E2E proof, it is a collection of well-tested libraries, not a platform.

# Architectural Intent
1. Start with a minimal proof: 3 panes, synthetic agent output, verify Robot Mode sees detection events.
2. Scale to realistic: 20 panes with mixed agent types, workflow triggers, policy gates, FTS search.
3. Scale to stress: 50+ panes with fleet memory engaged, prove tiered scrollback works under load.
4. Prove tx execution: ft tx run against real panes with policy gates and compensation.
5. For each scale tier, demonstrate the FULL loop: observe -> detect -> react -> audit.

# Acceptance Criteria
1. Minimal E2E (ft-d0ez0.1): ft watch + 3 panes + pattern detection + Robot Mode queries
2. 20-pane integration (ft-d0ez0.2): multi-agent detection, workflow, policy, FTS search
3. 50-pane stress (ft-d0ez0.3): fleet memory pressure transitions (Normal->Elevated->Critical), tiered scrollback eviction
4. Tx E2E (ft-d0ez0.4): ft tx run with real pane operations, compensation, audit trail
5. All tests produce structured JSON artifacts for reproducibility.
6. Tests run in CI or have clear manual reproduction instructions.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:35.214581Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:06:45.893656Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:06:45.893210Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","goal-line","integration","proof","swarm"]}
 {"id":"ft-d0ez0.1","title":"Minimal E2E proof: ft watch + 3 mock panes + pattern detection + Robot Mode verification","description":"# Goal
Create the smallest possible E2E test proving the full observation-detection-query loop works against real (or simulated) panes.

# Background
Every subsystem has strong unit tests (46,633 total), but nobody has proven the aggregate works: ft watch observing panes -> pattern engine detecting state transitions -> Robot Mode returning detection events. This is the foundational proof.

# Design
## Test script: tests/e2e/e2e_minimal_loop.py

### Pane output (use ACTUAL corpus fixture text, not paraphrases)
- Pane 0: codex.usage.reached — echo the text from corpus/codex/usage_reached.txt:
  "You've hit your usage limit. Please try again at 2026-01-20 12:34 UTC."
- Pane 1: claude_code pattern — echo the text from corpus/claude_code/usage_reached.txt:
  "rate limit reached; try again in 15 minutes"
- Pane 2: normal text with no pattern matches (e.g. "hello world")

### Steps
1. Create temp workspace dir with minimal ft.toml:
   [general]
   log_level = "debug"
   [ingest]
   poll_interval_ms = 200
   [patterns]
   packs = ["core"]
   [storage]
   db_path = "<tempdir>/ft.db"
2. Build ft binary (or use prebuilt from PATH)
3. Start ft watch --foreground --workspace <tempdir> as subprocess
4. Create 3 panes (headless mux server or WezTerm CLI)
5. Write corpus text to each pane
6. Wait for 2x poll_interval (400ms + 1s buffer = ~1.5s)
7. Run assertions via ft robot:
   a. ft robot state → assert ok:true, >= 3 panes
   b. ft robot events --limit 20 → assert >= 1 event with rule_id containing "usage" for pane 0 or 1
   c. ft robot search "usage limit" → assert >= 1 search result
   d. ft robot get-text <pane_0_id> --tail 5 → assert contains "hit your usage limit"
8. ft stop or SIGTERM the watcher
9. Cleanup tempdir

### Fallback for no-backend environments
- If neither WezTerm CLI nor headless mux server available: skip with exit code 77 (TAP skip) and message
- CI should ensure headless mux server is available

## Logging
- JSON report: tests/e2e/output/e2e_minimal_loop_<timestamp>.json
- Per-assertion: { check: "robot_state", expected: "ok=true, panes>=3", actual: <raw>, pass: bool, elapsed_ms: N }
- On failure: dump ft watch stderr, ft.db stat, all robot responses
- Python logging with JSON formatter to both file and stderr

# Acceptance Criteria
1. Script runs from clean state (no pre-existing ft.db).
2. All 4 Robot Mode assertions pass.
3. Completes in under 30 seconds.
4. Idempotent (cleanup on exit via atexit or try/finally).
5. JSON report produced.
6. On failure, output sufficient to debug without re-running.

# Implementation Notes
- Pattern rules codex.usage.reached and claude_code.usage.reached are in the built-in "core" pack.
- The headless mux server binary is crates/frankenterm-mux-server — check if it provides a PTY creation API or needs wezterm cli compatibility.
- Existing tests/e2e/ directory has Python scripts — follow existing patterns (check for test runner conventions).","notes":"Implemented tests/e2e/e2e_minimal_loop.py and verified the structured skip path via tests/e2e/output/e2e_minimal_loop_20260407T020831Z.json. Remaining validation of the live-pane success path is blocked on this machine because no usable ft binary is available locally and wezterm is not present in PATH. The script records both preconditions explicitly and exits 77 as designed.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:51.950550Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T05:00:43.138350Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T05:00:43.138183Z","close_reason":"Completed: remote offloaded build plus passing minimal E2E proof report","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","minimal","proof"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.1","depends_on_id":"ft-d0ez0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:51.950550Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2920,"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Binary discovery: The E2E script should locate the ft binary via: 1) FT_BINARY env var (explicit path), 2) cargo build -p frankenterm --release output, 3) which ft (PATH lookup). The script should fail early with a clear error if the binary is not found rather than failing obscurely during subprocess calls.","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:05:09Z"},{"id":2921,"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.1","author":"RedGate","text":"Implemented `tests/e2e/e2e_minimal_loop.py`.

Details:
- resolves `ft` as `FT_BINARY` -> cargo build outputs -> `which ft`
- reads the real codex and claude_code usage-reached corpus fixtures
- provisions an isolated temp workspace plus minimal `ft.toml`
- starts `ft watch --foreground` against that workspace
- drives three panes through WezTerm when available
- runs the required robot assertions (`state`, `events --limit 20`, `search "usage limit"`, `get-text <pane0> --tail 5`)
- writes a structured JSON report under `tests/e2e/output/`
- exits 77 with a structured skip report when the active backend is unavailable

Current environment note: local validation here cannot reach the live-pane path because `wezterm` is not in PATH. An `rch exec -- cargo build -p frankenterm --bin ft` offloaded build is in flight to validate real binary discovery before the script is exercised further.","created_at":"2026-04-07T02:01:26Z"},{"id":2922,"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.1","author":"RedGate","text":"Latest run: `python3 tests/e2e/e2e_minimal_loop.py` produced `tests/e2e/output/e2e_minimal_loop_20260407T020831Z.json` and exited `77`.

Skip artifact details:
- binary search order exercised and recorded: `FT_BINARY` -> cargo outputs -> `PATH`
- fixture paths recorded for both codex and claude_code usage corpus inputs
- combined skip reason captured: no usable `ft` binary local to this machine, and `wezterm` absent from `PATH`

The script is in place; what remains is success-path execution on a machine with both prerequisites available.","created_at":"2026-04-07T02:09:41Z"},{"id":2931,"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.1","author":"CopperHare","text":"CopperHare completion evidence:

- Patched `tests/e2e/e2e_minimal_loop.py` so the harness honors `FT_WEZTERM_CLI` consistently for backend discovery, pane spawn, and pane teardown.
- Offloaded build succeeded with explicit remote timeout increase: `env RCH_BUILD_TIMEOUT_SEC=1800 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_copperhare_ft-d0ez0-1 cargo build -p frankenterm --bin ft` on worker `vmi1264463`.
- Ran the live success-path proof on that worker against a simulated `wezterm` CLI bridge wired through `FT_WEZTERM_CLI` and the freshly built `/tmp/rch_target_copperhare_ft-d0ez0-1/debug/ft`.
- Report artifact: `tests/e2e/output/e2e_minimal_loop_20260407T045957Z.json`
- Result: `status=passed`; all required assertions were true for `robot state`, `robot events`, `robot search`, and `robot get-text`.

Notable runtime note from the passing run: `ft` selected the CLI backend because vendored metadata is currently considered stale (`vendored commit not recorded`), but that did not block the proof.","created_at":"2026-04-07T05:00:42Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-d0ez0.2","title":"20-pane integration test: multi-agent detection, workflow trigger, policy gate, FTS search verification","description":"# Goal
Scale the E2E proof to 20 panes with mixed agent types, verifying all major subsystems integrate correctly under realistic multi-agent conditions.

# Background
The minimal E2E (ft-d0ez0.1) proves the basic loop. This test proves it works at moderate scale with mixed agent types, workflow triggers, policy enforcement, and FTS search.

# Design
## Test script: tests/e2e/e2e_20pane_integration.py

### Setup
- 20 panes via headless mux server: 7 simulated Codex, 7 Claude Code, 3 Gemini, 3 plain shell
- Each agent pane receives fixture output from crates/frankenterm-core/tests/corpus/<agent>/
- Output mix: rate limits, errors, normal output, long-running ops

### Policy configuration (ft.toml for the test)
Pane 15 is policy-denied via safety config:
  [safety]
  require_prompt_active = false
  block_alt_screen = true
  [safety.rate_limits]
  send_text = { max_per_second = 0 }  # deny all sends to trigger rate limit
  # OR use a custom policy rule that denies pane_id=15 specifically

### Verification matrix (8 checks)
1. Delta extraction: ft robot get-text --all --tail 1 returns data for all 20 panes
2. Pattern detection: ft robot events --limit 50 returns rate-limit events for 17 agent panes
3. False positive check: ft robot events filtered to panes 17,18,19 returns ZERO events
4. Workflow trigger: ft workflow status shows at least 1 completed workflow execution
5. Policy gate: ft robot send 15 'test' returns ok:false with error.code=robot.policy_denied
6. FTS search: ft robot search 'rate limit' returns results spanning multiple panes
7. Robot Mode state: ft robot state returns all 20 panes with correct metadata (domain, title)
8. Audit trail: ft audit --decision deny shows the pane 15 denial record

### Timing
- Stagger pane output over 10 seconds to test temporal ordering
- Total duration target: under 60 seconds

## Logging
- JSON report: tests/e2e/output/e2e_20pane_<timestamp>.json
- Per-check: check_name, expected, actual, pass/fail, duration_ms, raw_response (first 500 chars)
- Summary: total_checks, passed, failed, elapsed_total_ms
- On failure: dump ft doctor --json, full event list, audit trail, failing check raw response

# Acceptance Criteria
1. All 8 verification checks pass.
2. Zero false-positive detections on plain shell panes.
3. At least 1 completed workflow in execution log.
4. Audit trail includes policy denial with pane_id=15.
5. Structured JSON report produced.
6. Completes in under 60 seconds.
7. On failure, diagnostic output pinpoints exactly which check failed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:57:05.594187Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T01:32:02.956948Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T01:32:02.956583Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","integration","multi-agent"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.2","depends_on_id":"ft-d0ez0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:57:05.594187Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.2","depends_on_id":"ft-d0ez0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:57:25.496062Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -63,7 +63,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-gofz5.2","title":"Create v0.1.0 tagged release with honest release notes and downloadable binaries","description":"# Goal
Cut the first real version release with honest release notes.

# Design
1. Version bump: Set version = "0.1.0" in relevant Cargo.toml files
2. Write release notes that honestly categorize:
   - Working: observation pipeline, pattern detection, Robot Mode, FTS search, policy engine, fleet memory, tiered scrollback, BOCPD, session persistence, connector SDK
   - Feature-gated: MCP, distributed mode, semantic search, web API, TUI
   - Partial: tx execution (orchestration framework complete, data plane in progress), mission dispatch
   - Known limitations: resource-intensive build, Rust nightly required, WezTerm backend dependency
3. Trigger release.yml workflow
4. Verify downloadable binaries are produced for Linux x86_64 and macOS arm64

# Acceptance Criteria
1. Git tag v0.1.0 exists with annotated release notes.
2. GitHub Release page has downloadable binaries.
3. Release notes are honest — no feature is listed as working unless it passes end-to-end.
4.  {"id":"ft-gofz5.3","title":"Add CI cargo-install smoke test that runs on every push to main","description":"# Goal
Add a CI job to .github/workflows/ci.yml that runs cargo install from the repo and verifies the binary works, catching install regressions early.

# Design
- New job in ci.yml: install-smoke
- Matrix: ubuntu-latest (x86_64), macos-latest (arm64)
- Steps: checkout → install Rust nightly → cargo install --path crates/frankenterm → ft --version → ft doctor --json
- Timeout: 30 minutes (the build is large)
- Cache: cargo registry + target dir for speed

# Acceptance Criteria
1. CI job passes on both platforms.
2. Job fails if ft binary doesn't produce valid --version output.
3. Job completes in under 20 minutes with caching.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:11.405247Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:01:54.765078Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:01:54.763741Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","regression","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5.3","depends_on_id":"ft-gofz5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:11.405247Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5.3","depends_on_id":"ft-gofz5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:15.917903Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-grz8r","title":"[EPIC] Eliminate verified first-party placeholders and stubs","description":"# Goal
Eliminate every verified first-party placeholder, stub, mock-like runtime branch, and misleading partial implementation surfaced by the 2026-04-02 audit.

Every in-scope item must end in exactly one of two states:
1. Real shipping behavior with tests, metrics, and operator-visible semantics.
2. Explicitly narrowed scope backed by code changes, docs, and guardrails so the code no longer masquerades as implemented behavior.

# Inventory Covered By This Epic
- CLI / operator support: `build_ntm_stub_response`, `chrono_stub_now`, scheduled backup compression fallback.
- Support crates: `frankenterm/env-bootstrap` and `ringlog` bootstrap/logger stubs.
- Client/runtime control plane: `ActiveWorkspaceChanged` TODO, unilateral-message FIXME, `spawn_pane` unimplemented, alt-screen hardcoded false.
- SDK / connectors / search: transparency verification stub, certification integration probe stub, generated Rust SDK transport unimplemented, `CrossEncoderReranker` no-op, `Model2VecEmbedder` stub, FastEmbed sentinel constructor behavior.
- Core runtime / UX: `NullPaneScrollbackAccess`, FTUI placeholder views, recording delta encodings, stale PaneCapabilities stub annotation.
- Rendering / windowing: Wayland seat+keyboard TODOs, WGL/macOS/EGL resize gaps, bitmap/WebGPU readback gaps, terminfo raw/cropped image gaps, GUI background/glyph placeholders.
- Mux / SSH: tmux PTY lifecycle gaps, detach unimplemented across domains, SFTP metadata mutation, SSH `Match Exec` evaluation.

# Scope Boundary
Only first-party source is in scope. Test doubles under `#[cfg(test)]`, intentional null objects, unsupported-platform no-op shims, and vendored upstream TODOs are handled only if this program reclassifies them with evidence.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:26:38.047585Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:31:29.254960Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:31:29.253673Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["placeholder-remediation","stub-audit"]}
-{"id":"ft-h164o","title":"[SEV] mcp_manifest_golden hardcodes 6 shared /tmp/ sqlite paths causing parallel test collisions","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_manifest_golden.rs lines 214, 347, 356, 372, 395, 435 each pass a fixed PathBuf like "/tmp/ft-mcp-manifest-no-db-subset.sqlite3" to capture_manifest(). Tests mcp_manifest_matches_golden_without_db, mcp_manifest_matches_golden_with_db, mcp_manifest_capture_is_deterministic, mcp_manifest_tool_names_are_unique, mcp_manifest_resource_uris_are_unique, mcp_manifest_tool_input_schemas_are_objects all share these global /tmp paths. Under concurrent agent builds (memory notes 50+ cargo processes, multiple /tmp/ft-<agent>-target dirs running the same test suite in parallel per workspace) two processes hitting the same sqlite3 path race on creation/registration. Even if build_server_with_db does not open the DB today (per the line 346 comment), any change that begins to open/init it turns these into flaky tests. This is exactly the anti-pattern testing-perfect-e2e-integration-tests-with-logging-and-no-mocks flags: hardcoded global temp paths instead of per-test tempdirs. Fix by using tempfile::TempDir or deriving paths from std::thread::current().id() + test name.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:02:16.960828Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:02:16.960828Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Tests mcp_manifest_matches_golden_without_db, mcp_manifest_matches_golden_with_db, mcp_manifest_capture_is_deterministic, mcp_manifest_tool_names_are_unique, mcp_manifest_resource_uris_are_unique, mcp_manifest_tool_input_schemas_are_objects all share these global /tmp paths. Under concurrent agent builds (memory notes 50+ cargo processes, multiple /tmp/ft-<agent>-target dirs running the same test suite in parallel per workspace) two processes hitting the same sqlite3 path race on creation/registration. Even if build_server_with_db does not open the DB today (per the line 346 comment), any change that begins to open/init it turns these into flaky tests. This is exactly the anti-pattern testing-perfect-e2e-integration-tests-with-logging-and-no-mocks flags: hardcoded global temp paths instead of per-test tempdirs. Fix by using tempfile::TempDir or deriving paths from std::thread::current().id() + test name.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:02:16.960828Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:28:52.225850Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-htf0i","title":"[HIGH] GUI resize increment saturation can overflow backend min-size arithmetic","description":"Commit 193a9ebb changes `ResizeIncrementCalculator` to saturate `base_width`/`base_height` at the u16 ceiling (`crates/frankenterm-gui/src/resize_increment_calculator.rs:15-35`). Multiple window backends then immediately compute `base + increment` in u16 without guarding overflow (`frankenterm/window/src/os/macos/window.rs:1410-1412`, `frankenterm/window/src/os/x11/window.rs:1892-1893`, `frankenterm/window/src/os/wayland/window.rs:915-916`). With large padding/border/tab-bar geometry, the new saturation can produce `base_width == u16::MAX`, so `base_width + x` overflows before min-size clamping. This turns an intended overflow hardening change into a runtime panic/wraparound path on oversized layouts. Expected fix: clamp stored base dimensions to `u16::MAX - increment` (or widen backend arithmetic) and add a regression test that asserts `base + increment` stays representable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:57:00.591671Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:45:12.138080Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:45:12.137486Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-jqvg5","title":"Resolve frankenterm-core clippy -D warnings gate","description":"# Workspace-level clippy gate

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 356 verification sweep. The
`frankenterm-core` crate reports **17 clippy errors** when run with
`-D warnings`:

    cargo clippy --no-deps -p frankenterm-core \\
        --features distributed,asupersync-runtime,web \\
        --lib --tests -- -D warnings

All 17 errors are in files **unrelated to ft-xbnl0.2.4**:

- `src/ipc.rs`
- `src/robot_sdk_contracts.rs`
- `src/runtime.rs`
- `src/snapshot_engine.rs`
- `src/ui_query.rs`
- `src/workflows/engine.rs`
- `src/workflows/runner.rs`

These are from recent commits by other agents.

## Why not fix in ft-xbnl0.2.4
AGENTS.md explicitly instructs "NEVER ... disturb in ANY way the work of
other agents." The violations may be:
- awaiting `#[allow(...)]` annotations from their owners
- pending intentional restructures
- already planned for cleanup

Touching them without coordination could override the owners' planned
solutions.

## Fix categories observed (examples)
- `clippy::duration-suboptimal-units` — `Duration::from_millis(2_000)` should be `from_secs(2)`
- `clippy::redundant-closure` — `|| crate::foo()` should just be `crate::foo`
- `clippy::semicolon-if-nothing-returned` — trailing unit-valued expression without semicolon
- Several others (see `cargo clippy` output at time of fix)

## Suggested approach
For each file, coordinate with the owning agent (via Agent Mail or by checking
recent git history for active work) before applying fixes. Prefer:
1. Fixing real issues over `#[allow(...)]` annotations.
2. Single-file commits so each fix is easy to review.
3. Running `ubs` on each file after the clippy fix to avoid introducing
   new bugs in the cleanup.

## Not a blocker for ft-xbnl0.2.4
The ft-xbnl0.2.4-scoped files (`src/distributed.rs`, `src/metrics.rs`,
`src/runtime_compat.rs`, `tests/web.rs`, `tests/ft_xbnl0_2_4_*.rs`) produce
zero clippy warnings.

See: `docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §6.1.1`.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FoggyRobin","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:04:55.194692Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:17:33.170677Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T15:17:33.170424Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["clippy","code-quality","workspace-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-jqvg5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:05:03.540304Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-kfkyi","title":"DistributedHttpClient must not transparently follow 3xx redirects","description":"# Security: DistributedHttpClient transparent redirect following

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 349 verification work. `DistributedHttpClient`
(built on the default asupersync HTTP client config) **follows 3xx redirects
transparently** by default. A 302 with an arbitrary `Location:` header is
followed without caller visibility.

Current behavior is pinned by `distributed_http_client_3xx_redirect_current_behavior_snapshot`
in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs` (tests module) — the test
asserts `Err` on a 302 whose Location is unresolvable, and explicitly
documents that assertion flipping is the trigger for this bead's work.

## Security concern
`DistributedHttpClient` is used for distributed node-to-node RPC (health
checks, event forwarding). Peer URLs are supposed to point at trusted
nodes. Transparent redirect following means:

- A compromised peer can respond `302 Location: http://attacker.com/` →
  next request body is sent to attacker (including event data, pane
  captures, auth tokens).
- Even without compromise, a misconfigured peer with a redirect loop
  could cause unbounded work.

## Fix
Configure `RedirectPolicy::none()` at the `DistributedHttpClient`
construction site — see `crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs`
around `DistributedHttpClient::new()` and `DistributedHttpClient::plaintext()`.

This forces the caller to explicitly handle (or ignore) the redirect.

## Verification
After the fix:
1. `distributed_http_client_3xx_redirect_current_behavior_snapshot` will
   fail — update it to assert `resp.status == 302` and rename to drop
   the "current-behavior-snapshot" suffix. Rename to
   `distributed_http_client_returns_3xx_redirect_as_ok_response`.
2. Add a second test asserting that a 302 → a different internal host
   is ALSO observable (not followed) — use a second TcpListener in
   the test so the "Location" resolves but the client still doesn't
   follow it.

## Out of scope
- Building a full redirect-following mechanism (if callers want redirects,
  they can opt in explicitly by following the Location themselves after
  inspecting it).
- The asupersync HTTP client's default RedirectPolicy — that's a library
  default and changing it would affect every other caller. The fix is
  to override at the FrankenTerm distributed-client construction site.

## References
- Finding tick: ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 349 (commit 4013f6e9)
- Related doc: docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §5 (out of scope carve-out)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-18T16:59:46.872931Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-18T17:08:05.852811Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T17:08:05.852502Z","close_reason":"Completed by tick 351 (commit 4717c434): DistributedHttpClient::new() now constructs via builder with .no_redirects() explicitly. 3xx responses are Ok(Response{status: 3xx}) observable to caller. Snapshot test renamed + flipped to assert Ok(302). All 40 ft-xbnl0.2.4 tests still pass via scripts/check_ft_xbnl0_2_4.sh.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","distributed","http","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-kfkyi","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-18T16:59:50.751738Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-mdaon","title":"[MEDIUM] escape-parser robustness suite contains vacuous CSI MR and missing concatenation property","description":"[MEDIUM] Recent commit f3242133 (test: proptest robustness suite for escape-parser) overstates the protection added by frankenterm/escape-parser/tests/proptest_parser_robustness.rs.

Evidence:
- The file header claims property 3: `Concatenation consistency — parse(a++b) == parse(a) ++ parse(b)` (lines 9-10), but there is no corresponding test anywhere in the file. The suite advertises a metamorphic relation it never checks.
- The new `csi_roundtrip_via_display` property is partially vacuous (lines 170-184). It only asserts equality when `actions1.len() == 1` AND `actions2.len() == 1`; otherwise it silently skips the case. A regression that starts emitting zero or multiple actions for generated CSI sequences would therefore pass this property instead of failing.
- The generated inputs in `arb_csi_sequence()` are always syntactically complete CSI byte strings (lines 30-47), so a non-singleton parse result is exactly the kind of behavioral drift this MR should catch, not skip.

Why this matters:
- This commit is framed as a robustness/metamorphic suite, but two of the strongest claimed relations are currently absent or weak enough to miss structural parser regressions.
- Escape parsing is a streaming state machine; vacuous MRs give false confidence around output-shape stability.

Requested fix:
- Add the missing concatenation-consistency property or remove the claim from the suite header/commit description.
- Tighten `csi_roundtrip_via_display` so generated complete CSI inputs must roundtrip to exactly one action on both parses, and fail otherwise.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:56:59.004870Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T05:59:30.401115Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-mejto","title":"MCP tool/resource manifest golden — protocol conformance freeze","description":"Freeze the MCP server's public protocol surface via a golden manifest test: tool names + description/input-schema hashes, resource URIs + description hashes, resource-template URIs + description hashes. Any tool addition, removal, description change, or input-schema change must explicitly update the golden. Prevents silent protocol drift for downstream MCP clients.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:23:19.218909Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T05:33:25.408221Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T05:33:25.408008Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","golden","mcp","testing"]}
 {"id":"ft-n2j5w","title":"Fix MCP module pre-existing test errors under default features and verify 170 test functions pass","description":"# Goal
Fix the known test compilation/runtime errors in mcp.rs that prevent the 170 test functions from running with default features enabled.

# Background
MEMORY.md documents: mcp.rs has pre-existing test errors with default features. The MCP module (3,229 lines, 170 #[test] functions) has comprehensive test coverage. MCP submodules (mcp_bridge, mcp_framework, mcp_helpers, etc.) are declared inside mcp.rs via #[path], NOT in lib.rs — this means feature-gate interactions may affect module resolution.

# Design
## Diagnostic steps
1. Run: cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib mcp 2>&1 | tee /tmp/mcp_errors.log
   (Using build recipe: CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-<agent>-target)
2. Categorize errors:
   - Type mismatches from recent module changes -> update test expectations
   - Missing types/imports due to feature gates -> add #[cfg(feature)] or fix import paths
   - Async runtime errors (#[tokio::test] vs asupersync) -> migrate to sync or runtime_compat
   - Stale test data referencing old API shapes -> update fixtures
3. Fix each error, verifying after each fix that the change compiles
4. Final run: cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib mcp -- all 170 tests green

## Common failure patterns to watch for
- MCP submodules use #[path] declarations — if a submodule file moves or renames, the path attribute breaks
- The 57 tokio-based test failures (ft-22x4r) may overlap — if MCP tests use #[tokio::test], coordinate with ft-682n6
- Feature flag interactions: some test helper types may only exist under specific features (mcp, subprocess-bridge, etc.)
- robot_types.rs changes may have updated MCP response envelope shapes without updating mcp.rs test expectations

## Structured Logging
- Diagnostic log: mcp_test_fix_<timestamp>.log with each error, category, fix applied
- Before/after test counts to verify no tests lost

# Acceptance Criteria
1. cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib mcp passes with 0 failures under default features.
2. All 170 test functions execute (none skipped, none #[ignore]d without documented reason).
3. No semantic changes to test intent — only update expectations to match current API.
4. Diagnostic log documenting each fix for future reference.
5. If any tests require non-default features, they must have explicit #[cfg(feature = "...")] annotations with comments explaining why.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:58:08.516219Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T03:18:06.705038Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T03:18:06.704865Z","close_reason":"Verified: 201 MCP tests pass with zero failures under default features (cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib mcp::tests). The pre-existing test errors documented in memory were likely fixed by other agents. No code changes needed — the 170 test functions mentioned in the bead have grown to 201, all passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fix","mcp","testing"]}
-{"id":"ft-n38nt","title":"mux proptest_tab_wire_serde test target fails to compile (PaneNode: Clone + 15-tuple prop_map)","description":"The `cargo test --manifest-path frankenterm/mux/Cargo.toml --test proptest_tab_wire_serde` target currently fails to compile at HEAD with two independent errors:

1. `error[E0277]: the trait bound PaneNode: Clone is not satisfied` at proptest_tab_wire_serde.rs:108-111 — `arb_pane_node`'s `Just(PaneNode::Empty)` and `arb_pane_entry().prop_map(PaneNode::Leaf)` require `PaneNode: Clone` for proptest's `Strategy` impl. `PaneNode` only derives `Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq, Debug` at frankenterm/mux/src/tab.rs:3906.

2. `error[E0599]: the method `prop_map` exists for tuple (..., × 15), but its trait bounds were not satisfied` at proptest_tab_wire_serde.rs:68 — `arb_pane_entry` builds a 15-element tuple but proptest 1.11's `Strategy` impl only extends to some smaller arity.

Both errors exist independently of ft-lapw8's split-test restoration. The test file has therefore been silently broken since at least commit 095ca88a. Surfacing this so the proptest suite is actually run in CI rather than silently failing to build.

Fix direction:
- Add `Clone` to `PaneNode` derive. `PaneEntry`, `SplitDirectionAndSize`, `SplitDirection`, `SplitSize`, and `Box<PaneNode>` already support Clone — the transitive closure is clean.
- Refactor `arb_pane_entry`'s 15-element tuple into a nested tuple (`((a,b,c,d,e,f,g), (h,i,j,k,l,m,n,o))`) or a fixture struct + `prop_map_each` to stay within proptest's tuple-arity limit.

Evidence: cargo test output at audit time shows 3 errors (8 error spans) under --no-default-features defaults with `CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-pane6-target`.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T06:20:56.034014Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:33:50.758543Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["codec","compile","mux","proptest","test-gap"]}

Both errors exist independently of ft-lapw8's split-test restoration. The test file has therefore been silently broken since at least commit 095ca88a. Surfacing this so the proptest suite is actually run in CI rather than silently failing to build.

 {"id":"ft-nnfwn","title":"[HIGH] MCP no-db manifest test does not actually freeze the advertised no-db surface","description":"Commit bba76a57 claims complete MCP manifest freezing, but `mcp_manifest_matches_golden_without_db` only checks that the tool array is non-empty, that names have a rough prefix shape, and that the JSON round-trips (`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_manifest_golden.rs:155-178`). It does not compare the no-db tool list against a golden, does not assert the promised strict-subset relation, and does not validate no-db resources or resource templates at all.
